title: Pleasure Is Not Theft
subtitle: An affirmation for the enjoyment that arrives with a side order of guilt
excerpt: A short affirmation for the pattern of enjoyment followed immediately by guilt. Rejects the premise that pleasure is taken from somewhere else, from productivity, from someone who deserves it more, and replaces it with a plain statement of permission.

practice.whenToUse: Use when enjoying something and the guilt is arriving alongside it.

content.text: A short affirmation for pleasure guilt: the pattern of enjoying something and immediately feeling you have taken something you weren't supposed to. Pleasure is not subtracted from a budget. It is not taken from someone else. It does not need to be earned before it arrives.
Say it when the pleasure arrives, or when the guilt follows it. Say it out loud. Once or three times.
Pleasure is not theft. I am allowed to be in this.

The first sentence is a factual counter to the guilt framing: pleasure is not taken from anyone or anything. The second is permission: this moment, this enjoyment, is allowed. Say them in order. The logic matters.
